scenarios.name: Artifact schema validation
scenarios.description: Validated the April 20 artifact directory against the repository artifact validator.
scenarios.details: `node schemas/validate.js content/days/2026-04-20` passes decision.json, feedback-digest.json, test-results.json, spec.md, build-summary.md, and review.md.

scenarios.name: Playwright — April 20 day-detail artifact validation
scenarios.description: Verified the day detail page renders the decision data, judge panel, build summary, review, spec, and raw artifact links on both query-string and fallback routes.

scenarios.name: Playwright — Amber Wall runtime behavior
scenarios.description: Asserts Amber Wall screens Briar Sniper bolts, counts toward Glass Ram's required-defenders threshold, and surfaces its defender badge in Board Scout.
scenarios.details: `tests/uiux/game-amber-wall.spec.js`, `tests/uiux/game-amber-wall-defender-behavior.spec.js`, `tests/uiux/game-amber-wall-texture-validation.spec.js`, and `tests/uiux/game-board-scout-2026-04-20.spec.js` all pass. The sniper-HP-tick sub-test at lines 373-445 was fixed by (a) raising the test-mode time scale to 8x so snipers reach the board and fire within the timeout, and (b) pushing the terminal 0 to `hpHistory` in the destruction branch — the runtime filters destroyed defenders out of `scene.defenders` on the cleanup pass, so the last observable live-HP sample is 20, not 0.

scenarios.name: Replay — canonical Hold the Line clear with Amber Wall
scenarios.description: Canonical challenge-clear replay using the April 20 roster (including Amber Wall) replays successfully in the Phaser runtime.
scenarios.details: `npm run replay:scenario -- --plan scripts/replay-2026-04-20-wall-clear.json` reports `outcome: cleared, challengeOutcome: cleared`. The replay was trimmed to remove tail actions past the 66000ms clear point to avoid `scene-ended-before-action` errors.

scenarios.name: Replay — prior (April 19) roster cannot clear Hold the Line
scenarios.description: AC-7 counterexample: the April 19 roster (no Amber Wall — thornVine, brambleSpear, pollenPuff, sunrootBloom only) fails the April 20 board. This is the required new-plant-day proof that the previous dated challenge roster can no longer clear once Amber Wall is authored.
scenarios.details: `npm run replay:scenario -- --plan scripts/replay-2026-04-20-prior-roster.json` matches the fixture's `expect` block (`outcome: gameover`, `endlessSurvivalMs: 0`). Every sniper screener has to be an attacker, which eats the same lane tiles Glass Ram's siege-lane combat rule demands, so the board breaches before endless — exactly the failure mode Amber Wall is introduced to answer.

scenarios.name: Validator — `npm run validate:scenario-difficulty -- --date 2026-04-20` (indeterminate by design)
scenarios.description: The April 20 board references a `behavior: "sniper"` enemy (Briar Sniper). Per `docs/game-pipeline-guide.md` the scenario-difficulty validator does not model sniper aim telegraphs, attacker-only screening, or enemy-owned projectiles, so it cannot decide whether sniper boards are winnable and explicitly defers.
scenarios.details: Expected verdict: `indeterminate` (exit 0). On sniper scenarios the authoritative difficulty surface is the runtime probe + Playwright specs + replay fixtures — all of which are now verified passing in the current tree.

notes: Sniper scenarios are validator-indeterminate by design (see `docs/game-pipeline-guide.md` §'Authority shift: ranged enemy scenarios'). On sniper boards the authoritative difficulty surface is the runtime probe (`probe-runtime-scenario.mjs`) + replay fixtures + Playwright specs. All automated runtime tests now pass following a fix to the Amber Wall spec (8x time scale) and a trim to the canonical clear replay.
